title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
text: progressify package works together futurize package. can parallelize add progress reporting single pipeline:

title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
text: BiocGenerics package defines generic functions lapply(), sapply(), mapply(), tapply(). S4 generic functions override non-generic, counterpart functions base package. BiocGenerics attached, solution specify base version wish progressify, .e.
code: y <- base::lapply(1:3, sqrt) |> progressify()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Henrik Bengtsson. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bengtsson H (2026). progressify: Progress Reporting Common Functions via One Magic Function. R package version 0.1.0-9001, https://progressify.futureverse.org.
code: @Manual{,   title = {progressify: Progress Reporting of Common Functions via One Magic Function},   author = {Henrik Bengtsson},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0-9001},   url = {https://progressify.futureverse.org}, }

title: Progress Reporting of Common Functions via One Magic Function
text: progressify package makes extremely simple report progress updates existing map-reduce calls. need know single function called progressify() take care everything, e.g. progressify() function signals progress updates via progressr package, meaning can choose among supported progressr handlers render progress updates, whether via terminal, progress bar, even sound. progressify package one hard dependency - progressr package. addition getting progress updates via progressr, using progressify() also get access benefits come progressr. Notably, full control progress reported, works seamlessly across different environments frontends.
code: y <- lapply(x, fcn) |> progressify() y <- map(x, fcn) |> progressify() y <- foreach(x = xs) %do% { fcn(x) } |> progressify()

title: Progress Reporting of Common Functions via One Magic Function
text: progressify package supports transpilation functions multiple packages. table summarizes supported map-reduce functions. programmatically see packages currently supported, use: see functions supported specific package, use: Table 1: Map-reduce functions currently supported progressify() progress reporting. examples:
code: progressify_supported_packages() progressify_supported_functions("purrr") library(progressify) handlers(global = TRUE)  xs <- 1:10 ys <- lapply(xs, function(x) { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) }) |> progressify()  d <- as.dendrogram(hclust(dist(USArrests))) d2 <- dendrapply(d, function(n) { Sys.sleep(0.01); n }) |> progressify()  xs <- 1:10 ys <- purrr::map(xs, function(x) { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) }) |> progressify()  xs <- list(1:5, 1:5) ys <- crossmap::xmap(xs, ~ .y * .x) |> progressify()  library(foreach) xs <- 1:10 ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) } |> progressify()  xs <- 1:10 ys <- plyr::llply(xs, function(x) { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) }) |> progressify()

title: Progress Reporting of Common Functions via One Magic Function
text: can also progressify calls growing set domain-specific CRAN packages optional built-support parallelization.

what: CRAN packages with support for progressify
title: Progress Reporting of Common Functions via One Magic Function
text: Table 2: CRAN packages domain-specific functions currently supported progressify() progress reporting. examples:
code: forest <- partykit::cforest(Survived ~ ., data = as.data.frame(Titanic), ntree = 50L) |> progressify()

title: Progress Reporting of Common Functions via One Magic Function
text: progressify package compatible futurize package, parallelizes code via futureverse. can combine two, either order, get progress reporting parallelization:
code: library(progressify) handlers(global = TRUE)  library(futurize) plan(multisession)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- lapply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |> futurize()  ys <- purrr::map(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |> futurize()  library(foreach) ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% { slow_fcn(x) } |> progressify() |> futurize()  ys <- plyr::llply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |> futurize()  forest <- partykit::cforest(dist ~ speed, data = cars, ntree = 50L) |> progressify() |> futurize()

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call with progress updates — progressify
code: progressify(   expr,   substitute = TRUE,   ...,   when = TRUE,   eval = TRUE,   envir = parent.frame() )

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call with progress updates — progressify
text: expr R expression. substitute TRUE, expr quoted. TRUE (default), expression progressified, otherwise . eval TRUE (default), progressified expression evaluated, otherwise returned. envir environment expr evaluated. ... used.

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call with progress updates — progressify
text: Returns value evaluated expression expr.

code: xs <- list(1, 1:2, 1:2, 1:5) y <- lapply(X = xs, FUN = sum) |> progressify() str(y) #> List of 4 #>  $ : num 1 #>  $ : int 3 #>  $ : int 3 #>  $ : int 15

text: package package name.

title: List packages and functions supporting progressification — progressify_supported_packages
code: pkgs <- progressify_supported_packages() pkgs #>  [1] "base"         "crossmap"     "doFuture"     "foreach"      "furrr"        #>  [6] "future.apply" "partykit"     "plyr"         "purrr"        "stats"         fcns <- progressify_supported_functions("base") print(fcns) #>  [1] ".mapply"   "Map"       "apply"     "by"        "eapply"    "lapply"    #>  [7] "mapply"    "replicate" "sapply"    "tapply"    "vapply"     if (requireNamespace("purrr")) {   fcns <- progressify_supported_functions("purrr")   print(fcns) } #>  [1] "imap"     "imap_chr" "imap_dbl" "imap_int" "imap_lgl" "imodify"  #>  [7] "map"      "map2"     "map2_chr" "map2_dbl" "map2_int" "map2_lgl" #> [13] "map2_vec" "map_chr"  "map_dbl"  "map_int"  "map_lgl"  "map_vec"  #> [19] "modify"   "modify2"  "pmap"     "pmap_chr" "pmap_dbl" "pmap_int" #> [25] "pmap_lgl" "pmap_vec" "pwalk"    "walk"     "walk2"

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
text: Transpile R expression

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
code: transpile(   expr,   options = list(...),   ...,   when = TRUE,   eval = TRUE,   envir = parent.frame(),   disable = FALSE,   type = "built-in",   what = "transpile",   unwrap = list(base::`{`, base::`(`, base::`!`, base::local, base::with, base::I,     base::identity, base::invisible, base::suppressMessages, base::suppressWarnings,     base::suppressPackageStartupMessages),   debug = FALSE )

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
text: expr R expression, typically function call transpile. FALSE, transpiler disabled, TRUE, re-enabled. NA, TRUE returned transpiler enabled, otherwise FALSE. options (optional) Named options transpilation. TRUE (default), expression transpiled, otherwise . eval TRUE (default), transpiled expression evaluated, otherwise returned. envir environment expression evaluated. type Type transpiler use. unwrap (optional) list functions considered wrapping functions transpiler unwrap ("enter"). allows us transpile expressions within { ... } local( ... ).

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
text: Returns value evaluated expression expr eval = TRUE, otherwise transpiled expression. expr NA, TRUE returned transpiler enabled, otherwise FALSE.

title: Version 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-07 retake previous, proof--concept version. Starting version, now borrowing futurize package, implements closely related “parallelization” transpilers.

title: Version 0.1.0
text: Add support map-reduce CRAN package future.apply, e.g. y <- future.apply::future_lapply(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support CRAN package crossmap, e.g. y <- crossmap::xmap(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support purrr, e.g. y <- purrr::map(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package furrr, e.g. y <- furrr::future_map(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package foreach, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %% { fcn(x) } |> progressify(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package doFuture, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %dofuture% { fcn(x) } |> progressify(). Add support plyr, e.g. y <- plyr::llply(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support replicate(), e.g. y <- replicate(n, rnorm(10)) |> progressify(). Add support base-R package stats, e.g. d2 <- dendrapply(d, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support domain-specific CRAN package partykit, e.g. y <- partykit::cforest(formula, data, ntree = 50L) |> progressify(). Add progressify_supported_packages() progressify_supported_functions() programmatically querying packages functions supported progressify().

title: Version 0.0.1
text: progressify() function makes common map-reduce functions report progress. Progress notifications relayed, among things. Add support base R, e.g. y <- lapply(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support purrr, e.g. y <- map(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support foreach, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %% { fcn(x) } |> progressify(). Add support plyr, e.g. y <- llply(xs, fcn) |> progressify().
